description: Digital proximity, ambient light, RGB, and gesture sensor, I2C

Global.name: RAM
quality_issues: address 0x00 not found in register summary table

fields.name: GEN
fields.description: Gesture Enable.  When asserted, the gesture state machine can be activated. Activation is subject to the states of PEN and GMODE bits.

fields.name: PIEN
fields.description: Proximity Interrupt Enable. When asserted, it permits proximity interrupts to be generated, subject to the persistence filter settings.

fields.name: AIEN
fields.description: ALS Interrupt Enable. When asserted, it permits ALS interrupts to be generated, subject to the persistence filter settings.

fields.name: WEN
fields.description: Wait Enable. This bit activates the wait feature. Writing a one activates the wait timer. Writing a zero disables the wait timer.

fields.name: PEN
fields.description: Proximity Detect Enable. This field activates the proximity detection. Writing a one activates the proximity. Writing a zero disables the proximity.

fields.name: AEN
fields.description: ALS Enable. This field activates ALS function. Writing a one activates the ALS. Writing a zero disables the ALS.

fields.name: PON
fields.description: Power ON. This field activates the internal oscillator to permit the timers and ADC channels to operate. Writing a one activates the oscillator. Writing a zero disables the oscillator and puts the device into a low power sleep mode. During reads and writes over the I2C interface, this bit is temporarily overridden and the oscillator is enabled, independent of the state of PON.

fields.name: ATIME
fields.description: Controls the internal integration time of ALS/Color analog to digital converters.

Global.name: ID Register
Global.description: The ID Register provides the device identification.

Global.name: Status Register
Global.description: The Status Register provides the status of the device.

fields.name: CPSAT
fields.description: Clear Photodiode Saturation. When asserted, the analog sensor was at the upper end of its dynamic range. The bit can be de-asserted by sending a Clear channel interrupt command (0xE6 CICLEAR) or by disabling the ADC (AEN=0). This bit triggers an interrupt if CPSIEN is set.

fields.name: PGSAT
fields.description: Indicates that an analog saturation event occurred during a previous proximity or gesture cycle. Once set, this bit remains set until cleared by clear proximity interrupt special function command (0xE5 PICLEAR) or by disabling Prox (PEN=0). This bit triggers an interrupt if PSIEN is set.

fields.name: PINT
fields.description: Proximity Interrupt. This bit triggers an interrupt if PIEN in ENABLE is set.

fields.name: AINT
fields.description: ALS Interrupt. This bit triggers an interrupt if AIEN in ENABLE is set.

fields.name: GINT
fields.description: Gesture Interrupt. GINT is asserted when GFVLV becomes greater than GFIFOTH or if GVALID has become asserted when GMODE transitioned to zero. The bit is reset when FIFO is completely emptied (read).

fields.name: PVALID
fields.description: Proximity Valid. Indicates that a proximity cycle has completed since PEN was asserted or since PDATA was last read. A read of PDATA automatically clears PVALID.

fields.name: AVALID
fields.description: ALS Valid. Indicates that an ALS cycle has completed since AEN was asserted or since a read from any of the ALS/Color data registers.

ALS.name: ATIME
quality_issues: identical field layout as adjacent register 0x81

ALS.name: AILTL
ALS.description: ALS interrupt low threshold low byte

fields.name: AILTL
fields.description: ALS interrupt low threshold low byte

ALS.name: AILTH
ALS.description: ALS interrupt low threshold high byte

fields.name: AILTH
fields.description: ALS interrupt low threshold high byte

ALS.name: AIHTL
ALS.description: ALS interrupt high threshold low byte

fields.name: AIHTL
fields.description: ALS interrupt high threshold low byte

ALS.name: AIHTH
ALS.description: ALS interrupt high threshold high byte

fields.name: AIHTH
fields.description: ALS interrupt high threshold high byte

ALS.name: CDATAL
ALS.description: The CDATAL Register contains the low byte of clear channel data.

fields.name: CDATAL
fields.description: Low Byte of clear channel data.

ALS.name: RDATAL
ALS.description: The RDATAL Register contains the low byte of red channel data.

fields.name: RDATAL
fields.description: Low Byte of red channel data.

ALS.name: GDATAL
ALS.description: The GDATAL Register contains the low byte of green channel data.

fields.name: GDATAL
fields.description: Low Byte of green channel data.

ALS.name: BDATAL
ALS.description: The BDATAL Register contains the low byte of blue channel data.

fields.name: BDATAL
fields.description: Low Byte of blue channel data.

Proximity.name: CONFIG3
Proximity.description: Configuration register 3 for proximity photodiode selection

Gesture.name: GPENTH
Gesture.description: Gesture proximity enter threshold high byte

fields.name: GPENTH
fields.description: Gesture proximity enter threshold high byte

Gesture.name: Gesture Exit Threshold Register
Gesture.description: The Gesture Exit Threshold Register sets the threshold for gesture exit.

fields.name: GEXTH
fields.description: Gesture Exit Threshold. This register sets the threshold value used to determine a “gesture end” and subsequent exit of the gesture state machine. Setting GTHR_OUT to 0x00 will prevent gesture exit until GMODE is set to 0.

Gesture.name: Gesture Configuration One Register
Gesture.description: The Gesture Configuration One Register contains settings that govern gesture detector masking and FIFO interrupt generation.

fields.name: GFIFOTH
fields.description: Gesture FIFO Threshold. This value is compared with the FIFO Level (i.e. the number of UDLR datasets) to generate an interrupt (if enabled).

fields.name: GEXMSK
fields.description: Gesture Exit Mask. Controls which of the gesture detector photodiodes (UDLR) will be included to determine a “gesture end” and subsequent exit of the gesture state machine. Unmasked UDLR data will be compared with the value in GTHR_OUT. Field value bits correspond to UDLR detectors.

fields.name: GEXPERS
fields.description: Gesture Exit Persistence. When a number of consecutive “gesture end” occurrences become equal or greater to the GEPERS value, the Gesture state machine is exited.

Gesture.name: GCONF2
Gesture.description: Gesture Configuration Two register. Contains settings for wait time, LDR drive current strength and Gesture gain control.

fields.name: GGAIN
fields.description: Gesture Gain Control. Sets the gain of the proximity receiver in gesture mode.

fields.name: GLDRIVE
fields.description: Gesture LED Drive Strength. Sets LED Drive Strength in gesture mode.

fields.name: GWTIME
fields.description: Gesture Wait Time. Controls the amount of time in a low power mode between gesture detection cycles.

Gesture.name: GPULSE
Gesture.description: Gesture Pulse Count and Length register. Sets Pulse Width Modified current during a Gesture Pulse and number of pulses on LDR pin.

fields.name: GPLEN
fields.description: Gesture Pulse Length. Sets the LED_ON pulse width during a Gesture LDR Pulse.

fields.name: GPULSE
fields.description: Number of Gesture Pulses. Specifies the number of pulses to be generated on LDR. Number of pulses is set by GPULSE value plus 1.

Gesture.name: GCONF3
Gesture.description: Gesture Configuration Three Register. Controls which gesture photodiode pairs are enabled.

fields.name: GDIMS
fields.description: Gesture Dimension Select. 0=Both pairs active, 1=UP-DOWN only, 2=LEFT-RIGHT only, 3=Both pairs active.

Gesture.name: GCONF4
Gesture.description: Gesture Configuration Four Register. Controls gesture interrupts and mode.

Gesture.name: GFLVL
Gesture.description: Gesture FIFO Level Register. Indicates the number of datasets that are currently available in the FIFO for read. Reading a complete FIFO dataset (from address 0xFC to 0xFF) constitutes the reduction of the GPENTH register by one.

Gesture.name: GSTATUS
Gesture.description: Gesture Status Register. Indicates gesture state machine status.

fields.name: GFOV
fields.description: Gesture FIFO Overflow. 1=FIFO full, data lost.
